data: Haluntium honours the son of Timandros
Quadrangular statue block, cut down across the top and with some damage to the left and right sides.
Three lines of ancient Greek. The first line is partially lost across the top, the second and third are damaged at the left and right margins.

The letters are wide, well spaced and regular in form, and deeply incised. Alpha with straight bar; epsilon has short middle bar; kappa has full length arms; mu has vertical first and last, with middle strokes almost full height; omicron is full size; rho has large rounded head, closed; sigma has parallel top and bottom, and middle strokes almost full width; omega is closed, and slightly tear-drop shaped, with horizontal tails.
Original discovery not recorded, first published by Salinas in 1880.
Photo J. Prag, 2022-05-23, courtesy  Museo Archeologico Regionale Antonino Salinas
Photo J. Prag, courtesy  Museo Archeologico Regionale Antonino Salinas
Partially reopened following extensive restoration work. Comprehensive collection of archaeological material, predominantly from the West of the island, with an important Etruscan collection.
Centre of Palermo, Piazza Olivella 24, 90100 - Palermo
Inscriptiones Graecae Siciliae et Italiae, additis graecis Galliae Hispaniae, Britanniae, Germaniae inscriptionibus

G. Kaibel, Inscriptiones Graecae Siciliae et Italiae, additis graecis Galliae Hispaniae, Britanniae, Germaniae inscriptionibus, Inscriptiones Graecae consilio et auctoritate Academiae Litterarum Regiae Borussicae Editae. Volumen XIV., XIV (Berlin: Georgius Reimerus, 1890)
Iscrizioni greche lapidarie del Museo di Palermo
M.T. Manni Piraino, Iscrizioni greche lapidarie del Museo di Palermo, Sikelika 6 (Palermo: S. F. Flaccovio, 1973)

Accademia nazionale dei Lincei, Istituto nazionale di archeologia e storia dell’arte (Italy), e Reale Accademia d’Italia, «Notizie degli scavi di antichità», Notizie degli Scavi di Antichità, 1876, http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/1646037
Koinai et Koiné dans la documentation épigraphique de l'Italie méridionale
C. Consani, « Koinai et Koiné dans la documentation épigraphique de l’Italie méridionale », in La Koié grecque antique. II. La concurrence, éd. par C. Brixhe, 1996, 113‑32
Consani, 1996, Koinai et Koiné dans la documentation épigraphique de l'Italie méridionale

1          ΟΔΑΜΟΣ       
2.ΙΩΤΟΝΤΙΜΑΝΔΡΟ[.]
3ΕΥΝΟΙΑΣΕΝΕΚΕ.

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text based upon autopsy ;
  • 2: Salinas: [Κ]ίωτον; Kaibel: [Β]ίωτον; lapis: trace of upper eye of Β (contra Salinas, trace of K).

Italian translation

Norma Quagliana

Il damos (onora) Biotos figlio di Timandros per benevolenza

English translation

Jonathan Prag

The people (honours) Biotos son of Timandros, for the sake of goodwill

commentary

Notwithstanding the damage, it is clear that there was no ethnic included in the first part of the text, and the inscription appears complete. The name Βίωτος is also attested on tile and amphora stamps from southern Italy; no other form ending -ιωτος is known from the region.
